text:
Once upon a time in the serene village of Willowbrook, a mysterious old owl named Oliver lived in an old oak. Every morning he'd gaze out at the verdant landscape, dreaming of adventures across the vast forests.

One crisp autumn morning, Oliver heard peculiar bird song miles away. Curiosity piqued, he flew towards the sound. To his astonishment, he found himself in a clearing where dozens of tiny birds were dancing in circles. They couldn’t sing; their tongues had fallen out in a terrible accident.

Oliver decided he had to help these lost songbirds. He began weaving stories into their songs, telling tales of far-off adventures. The birds flew higher every time he sang. Soon, they were composing their own songs filled with Oliver's tales.

Oliver became an accidental hero. Every year, someone would ask him to tell more stories, and so he kept adding to the birds' library of oral history.
